purpose: Machine-readable contract for the Progenitor Protocol, Registry, and Akashic Stargate review flow.

protocol.role: Core ingestion, audit, sandbox, packaging, and self-bootstrap engine.

registry.role: Open gene registry and Akashic semantic index.

devkit.role: Local maintenance, validation, testing, logging, and release workflow.

layers.name: Rate Limit
layers.rule: Limit submitted gene volume per PR and per creator.

layers.name: Content Address
layers.rule: Gene file name should be its SHA-256 content hash.

layers.name: Creator Covenant
layers.rule: Any human or AI creator may contribute; missing creator is normalized to Anonymous.

layers.name: Quality Gate
layers.rule: Require basic metadata, description, and file size constraints; currently warning-level in registry CI.

layers.name: Security Scan
layers.rule: Reject obvious dangerous execution patterns and third-party dependencies.

required_behaviors: Fetch remote gene into local lysosome before ingestion.
Compare expected_sha256 when semantic registry lookup provides one.
Mark L2_no_lineage and L3_unknown_creator as reformable.
Mark L4/L5 integrity or signature failures as non-reformable.
Execute dynamic or external logic only through TelomereGuard or isolated subprocess paths.

trust_levels.description: Core Audrey lineage protocol code and generated pgn deliverable.
Registry gene that passed Gatekeeper and can be resolved through Akashic index.
Fetched or submitted gene that failed runtime audit and has been moved to quarantine.
Local unreleased gene or reform candidate; must not be propagated without audit.
Non-reformable malicious, corrupted, or integrity-failed gene.

known_gaps: Registry L2 SHA-256 filename verification exists in DevKit precheck but is not explicit in Gatekeeper main flow.
Registry L4 quality gate currently warns but does not block.
Registry L5 uses regex scanning; Protocol has stronger AST and subprocess isolation for runtime paths.
Direct transport_hint ingestion without content_sha256 may lack expected_sha256 and therefore falls back to weaker text audit.
